NKJV.1: Wine is a mocker, Strong drink is a brawler, And whoever is led astray by it is not wise.
NKJV.2: The wrath of a king is like the roaring of a lion; Whoever provokes him to anger sins against his own life.
NKJV.3: It is honorable for a man to stop striving, Since any fool can start a quarrel.
NKJV.4: The lazy man will not plow because of winter; He will beg during harvest and have nothing.
NKJV.5: Counsel in the heart of man is like deep water, But a man of understanding will draw it out.
NKJV.6: Most men will proclaim each his own goodness, But who can find a faithful man?
NKJV.7: The righteous man walks in his integrity; His children are blessed after him.
NKJV.8: A king who sits on the throne of judgment Scatters all evil with his eyes.
NKJV.9: Who can say, “I have made my heart clean, I am pure from my sin”?
NKJV.10: Diverse weights and diverse measures, They are both alike, an abomination to the LORD.
NKJV.11: Even a child is known by his deeds, Whether what he does is pure and right.
NKJV.12: The hearing ear and the seeing eye, The LORD has made them both.
NKJV.13: Do not love sleep, lest you come to poverty; Open your eyes, and you will be satisfied with bread.
NKJV.14: “It is good for nothing,” cries the buyer; But when he has gone his way, then he boasts.
NKJV.15: There is gold and a multitude of rubies, But the lips of knowledge are a precious jewel.
NKJV.16: Take the garment of one who is surety for a stranger, And hold it as a pledge when it is for a seductress.
NKJV.17: Bread gained by deceit is sweet to a man, But afterward his mouth will be filled with gravel.
NKJV.18: Plans are established by counsel;  By wise counsel wage war.
NKJV.19: He who goes about as a talebearer reveals secrets; Therefore do not associate with one who flatters with his lips.
NKJV.20: Whoever curses his father or his mother,  His lamp will be put out in deep darkness.
NKJV.21: An inheritance gained hastily at the beginning  Will not be blessed at the end.
NKJV.22: Do not say, “I will recompense evil”;  Wait for the LORD, and He will save you.
NKJV.23: Diverse weights are an abomination to the LORD, And dishonest scales are not good.
NKJV.24: A man’s steps are of the LORD; How then can a man understand his own way?
NKJV.25: It is a snare for a man to devote rashly something as holy, And afterward to reconsider his vows.
NKJV.26: A wise king sifts out the wicked, And brings the threshing wheel over them.
NKJV.27: The spirit of a man is the lamp of the LORD, Searching all the inner depths of his heart.
NKJV.28: Mercy and truth preserve the king, And by lovingkindness he upholds his throne.
NKJV.29: The glory of young men is their strength, And the splendor of old men is their gray head.
NKJV.30: Blows that hurt cleanse away evil, As do stripes the inner depths of the heart.

ESV.1: Wine is a mocker, strong drink a brawler, and whoever is led astray by it is not wise.
ESV.2: The terror of a king is like the growling of a lion; whoever provokes him to anger forfeits his life.
ESV.3: It is an honor for a man to keep aloof from strife, but every fool will be quarreling.
ESV.4: The sluggard does not plow in the autumn; he will seek at harvest and have nothing.
ESV.5: The purpose in a man's heart is like deep water, but a man of understanding will draw it out.
ESV.6: Many a man proclaims his own steadfast love, but a faithful man who can find?
ESV.7: The righteous who walks in his integrity — blessed are his children after him!
ESV.8: A king who sits on the throne of judgment winnows all evil with his eyes.
ESV.9: Who can say, “I have made my heart pure; I am clean from my sin”?
ESV.10: Unequal  weights and unequal measures are both alike an abomination to the LORD.
ESV.11: Even a child makes himself known by his acts, by whether his conduct is pure and upright.
ESV.12: The hearing ear and the seeing eye, the LORD has made them both.
ESV.13: Love not sleep, lest you come to poverty; open your eyes, and you will have plenty of bread.
ESV.14: “Bad, bad,” says the buyer, but when he goes away, then he boasts.
ESV.15: There is gold and abundance of costly stones, but the lips of knowledge are a precious jewel.
ESV.16: Take a man's garment when he has put up security for a stranger, and hold it in pledge when he puts up security for foreigners.
ESV.17: Bread gained by deceit is sweet to a man, but afterward his mouth will be full of gravel.
ESV.18: Plans are established by counsel; by wise guidance wage war.
ESV.19: Whoever goes about slandering reveals secrets; therefore do not associate with a simple babbler.
ESV.20: If one curses his father or his mother, his lamp will be put out in utter darkness.
ESV.21: An inheritance gained hastily in the beginning will not be blessed in the end.
ESV.22: Do not say, “I will repay evil”; wait for the LORD, and he will deliver you.
ESV.23: Unequal weights are an abomination to the LORD, and false scales are not good.
ESV.24: A man's steps are from the LORD; how then can man understand his way?
ESV.25: It is a snare to say rashly, “It is holy,” and to reflect only after making vows.
ESV.26: A wise king winnows the wicked and drives the wheel over them.
ESV.27: The spirit  of man is the lamp of the LORD, searching all his innermost parts.
ESV.28: Steadfast love and faithfulness preserve the king, and by steadfast love his throne is upheld.
ESV.29: The glory of young men is their strength, but the splendor of old men is their gray hair.
ESV.30: Blows that wound cleanse away evil; strokes make clean the innermost parts.

NASB.1: Wine is a mocker, strong drink a brawler, And whoever is intoxicated by it is not wise.
NASB.2: The terror of a king is like the growling of a lion; He who provokes him to anger forfeits his own life.
NASB.3: Keeping away from strife is an honor for a man, But any fool will quarrel.
NASB.4: The sluggard does not plow after the autumn, So he begs during the harvest and has nothing.
NASB.5: A plan in the heart of a man is [like] deep water, But a man of understanding draws it out.
NASB.6: Many a man proclaims his own loyalty, But who can find a trustworthy man?
NASB.7: A righteous man who walks in his integrity-- How blessed are his sons after him.
NASB.8: A king who sits on the throne of justice Disperses all evil with his eyes.
NASB.9: Who can say, 'I have cleansed my heart, I am pure from my sin '?
NASB.10: Differing weights and differing measures, Both of them are abominable to the LORD.
NASB.11: It is by his deeds that a lad distinguishes himself If his conduct is pure and right.
NASB.12: The hearing ear and the seeing eye, The LORD has made both of them.
NASB.13: Do not love sleep, or you will become poor; Open your eyes, [and] you will be satisfied with food.
NASB.14: 'Bad, bad,' says the buyer, But when he goes his way, then he boasts.
NASB.15: There is gold, and an abundance of jewels; But the lips of knowledge are a more precious thing.
NASB.16: Take his garment when he becomes surety for a stranger; And for foreigners, hold him in pledge.
NASB.17: Bread obtained by falsehood is sweet to a man, But afterward his mouth will be filled with gravel.
NASB.18: Prepare plans by consultation, And make war by wise guidance.
NASB.19: He who goes about as a slanderer reveals secrets, Therefore do not associate with a gossip.
NASB.20: He who curses his father or his mother, His lamp will go out in time of darkness.
NASB.21: An inheritance gained hurriedly at the beginning Will not be blessed in the end.
NASB.22: Do not say, 'I will repay evil'; Wait for the LORD, and He will save you.
NASB.23: Differing weights are an abomination to the LORD, And a false scale is not good.
NASB.24: Man's steps are [ordained] by the LORD, How then can man understand his way?
NASB.25: It is a trap for a man to say rashly, 'It is holy!' And after the vows to make inquiry.
NASB.26: A wise king winnows the wicked, And drives the [threshing] wheel over them.
NASB.27: The spirit of man is the lamp of the LORD, Searching all the innermost parts of his being.
NASB.28: Loyalty and truth preserve the king, And he upholds his throne by righteousness.
NASB.29: The glory of young men is their strength, And the honor of old men is their gray hair.
NASB.30: Stripes that wound scour away evil, And strokes [reach] the innermost parts.

NLT.1:
Wine produces mockers; alcohol leads to brawls.
Those led astray by drink cannot be wise.
NLT.2:
The king’s fury is like a lion’s roar;
to rouse his anger is to risk your life.
NLT.3:
Avoiding a fight is a mark of honor;
only fools insist on quarreling.
NLT.4:
Those too lazy to plow in the right season
will have no food at the harvest.
NLT.5:
Though good advice lies deep within the heart,
a person with understanding will draw it out.
NLT.6:
Many will say they are loyal friends,
but who can find one who is truly reliable?
NLT.7:
The godly walk with integrity;
blessed are their children who follow them.
NLT.8:
When a king sits in judgment, he weighs all the evidence,
distinguishing the bad from the good.
NLT.9:
Who can say, “I have cleansed my heart;
I am pure and free from sin”?
NLT.10:
False weights and unequal measures —
the LORD detests double standards of every kind.
NLT.11:
Even children are known by the way they act,
whether their conduct is pure, and whether it is right.
NLT.12:
Ears to hear and eyes to see —
both are gifts from the LORD.
NLT.13:
If you love sleep, you will end in poverty.
Keep your eyes open, and there will be plenty to eat!
NLT.14:
The buyer haggles over the price, saying, “It’s worthless,”
then brags about getting a bargain!
NLT.15:
Wise words are more valuable
than much gold and many rubies.
NLT.16:
Get security from someone who guarantees a stranger’s debt.
Get a deposit if he does it for foreigners.
NLT.17:
Stolen bread tastes sweet,
but it turns to gravel in the mouth.
NLT.18:
Plans succeed through good counsel;
don’t go to war without wise advice.
NLT.19:
A gossip goes around telling secrets,
so don’t hang around with chatterers.
NLT.20:
If you insult your father or mother,
your light will be snuffed out in total darkness.
NLT.21:
An inheritance obtained too early in life
is not a blessing in the end.
NLT.22:
Don’t say, “I will get even for this wrong.”
Wait for the LORD to handle the matter.
NLT.23:
The LORD detests double standards;
he is not pleased by dishonest scales.
NLT.24:
The LORD directs our steps,
so why try to understand everything along the way?
NLT.25:
Don’t trap yourself by making a rash promise to God
and only later counting the cost.
NLT.26:
A wise king scatters the wicked like wheat,
then runs his threshing wheel over them.
NLT.27:
The LORD’s light penetrates the human spirit,
exposing every hidden motive.
NLT.28:
Unfailing love and faithfulness protect the king;
his throne is made secure through love.
NLT.29:
The glory of the young is their strength;
the gray hair of experience is the splendor of the old.
NLT.30:
Physical punishment cleanses away evil;
such discipline purifies the heart.

WEB.1: Wine is a mocker, and beer is a brawler. Whoever is led astray by them is not wise.
WEB.2: The terror of a king is like the roaring of a lion. He who provokes him to anger forfeits his own life.
WEB.3: It is an honor for a man to keep aloof from strife; but every fool will be quarreling.
WEB.4: The sluggard will not plow by reason of the winter; therefore he shall beg in harvest, and have nothing.
WEB.5: Counsel in the heart of man is like deep water; but a man of understanding will draw it out.
WEB.6: Many men claim to be men of unfailing love, but who can find a faithful man?
WEB.7: A righteous man walks in integrity. Blessed are his children after him.
WEB.8: A king who sits on the throne of judgment scatters away all evil with his eyes.
WEB.9: Who can say, “I have made my heart pure. I am clean and without sin?”
WEB.10: Differing weights and differing measures, both of them alike are an abomination to Yahweh.
WEB.11: Even a child makes himself known by his doings, whether his work is pure, and whether it is right.
WEB.12: The hearing ear, and the seeing eye, Yahweh has made even both of them.
WEB.13: Don’t love sleep, lest you come to poverty. Open your eyes, and you shall be satisfied with bread.
WEB.14: “It’s no good, it’s no good,” says the buyer; but when he is gone his way, then he boasts.
WEB.15: There is gold and abundance of rubies; but the lips of knowledge are a rare jewel.
WEB.16: Take the garment of one who puts up collateral for a stranger; and hold him in pledge for a wayward woman.
WEB.17: Fraudulent food is sweet to a man, but afterwards his mouth is filled with gravel.
WEB.18: Plans are established by advice; by wise guidance you wage war!
WEB.19: He who goes about as a tale-bearer reveals secrets; therefore don’t keep company with him who opens wide his lips.
WEB.20: Whoever curses his father or his mother, his lamp shall be put out in blackness of darkness.
WEB.21: An inheritance quickly gained at the beginning, won’t be blessed in the end.
WEB.22: Don’t say, “I will pay back evil.” Wait for Yahweh, and he will save you.
WEB.23: Yahweh detests differing weights, and dishonest scales are not pleasing.
WEB.24: A man’s steps are from Yahweh; how then can man understand his way?
WEB.25: It is a snare to a man to make a rash dedication, then later to consider his vows.
WEB.26: A wise king winnows out the wicked, and drives the threshing wheel over them.
WEB.27: The spirit of man is Yahweh’s lamp, searching all his innermost parts.
WEB.28: Love and faithfulness keep the king safe. His throne is sustained by love.
WEB.29: The glory of young men is their strength. The splendor of old men is their gray hair.
WEB.30: Wounding blows cleanse away evil, and beatings purge the innermost parts.

CSB17.1:
Wine is a mocker, ⓝ beer is a brawler;
whoever goes astray [1] because of them is not wise.
CSB17.2:
A king’s terrible wrath is like the roaring of a lion;
anyone who provokes him endangers himself. ⓐ
CSB17.3:
Honor belongs to the person who ends a dispute, ⓑ
but any fool can get himself into a quarrel. ⓒ
CSB17.4:
The slacker does not plow during planting season; [2]
at harvest time he looks, [3] and there is nothing. ⓓ
CSB17.5:
Counsel in a person’s heart is deep water; ⓔ
but a person of understanding draws it out.
CSB17.6:
Many a person proclaims his own loyalty,
but who can find a trustworthy person? ⓕ
CSB17.7:
A righteous person acts with integrity; ⓖ
his children who come after him will be happy. ⓗ
CSB17.8:
A king sitting on a throne to judge
separates out all evil with his eyes. ⓘ
CSB17.9:
Who can say, “I have kept my heart pure;
I am cleansed from my sin”? ⓙ
CSB17.10:
Differing weights and varying measures [4]  —
both are detestable to the LORD. ⓚ
CSB17.11:
Even a young man is known by his actions —
by whether his behavior is pure and upright. ⓛ
CSB17.12:
The hearing ear and the seeing eye —
the LORD made them both. ⓜ
CSB17.13:
Don’t love sleep, or you will become poor;
open your eyes, and you’ll have enough to eat. ⓝ
CSB17.14:
“It’s worthless, it’s worthless!” the buyer says,
but after he is on his way, he gloats.
CSB17.15:
There is gold and a multitude of jewels,
but knowledgeable lips are a rare treasure. ⓐ
CSB17.16:
Take his garment,
for he has put up security for a stranger;
get collateral if it is for foreigners. ⓑ
CSB17.17:
Food gained by fraud is sweet to a person, ⓒ
but afterward his mouth is full of gravel.
CSB17.18:
Finalize plans with counsel, ⓓ
and wage war with sound guidance. ⓔ
CSB17.19:
The one who reveals secrets is a constant gossip; ⓕ
avoid someone with a big mouth. ⓖ
CSB17.20:
Whoever curses his father or mother —  ⓗ
his lamp will go out in deep darkness. ⓘ
CSB17.21:
An inheritance gained prematurely
will not be blessed ultimately. ⓙ
CSB17.22:
Don’t say, “I will avenge this evil!” ⓚ
Wait on the LORD, ⓛ and he will rescue you. ⓜ
CSB17.23:
Differing weights [5] are detestable to the LORD, ⓝ
and dishonest scales ⓐ are unfair.
CSB17.24:
Even a courageous person’s steps are determined by the LORD, ⓑ
so how can anyone understand his own way?
CSB17.25:
It is a trap for anyone to dedicate something rashly
and later to reconsider his vows. ⓒ
CSB17.26:
A wise king separates out the wicked ⓓ
and drives the threshing wheel ⓔ over them.
CSB17.27:
The LORD’s lamp sheds light on a person’s life, [6]
searching the innermost parts. [7] ⓕ
CSB17.28:
Loyalty and faithfulness guard a king;
through loyalty he maintains his throne.
CSB17.29:
The glory of young men is their strength,
and the splendor of old men is gray hair. ⓖ
CSB17.30:
Lashes and wounds purge away evil, ⓗ
and beatings cleanse the innermost parts. [8]

AMP.1:
Wine is a mocker, strong drink a riotous brawler;
And whoever is intoxicated by it is not wise.
AMP.2:
The terror of a king is like the roaring of a lion;
Whoever provokes him to anger forfeits his own life.
AMP.3:
It is an honor for a man to keep away from strife [by handling situations with thoughtful foresight],
But any fool will [start a] quarrel [without regard for the consequences].
AMP.4:
The lazy man does not plow when the winter [planting] season arrives;
So he begs at the [next] harvest and has nothing [to reap].
AMP.5:
A plan (motive, wise counsel) in the heart of a man is like water in a deep well,
But a man of understanding draws it out.
AMP.6:
Many a man proclaims his own loyalty and goodness,
But who can find a faithful and trustworthy man?
AMP.7:
The righteous man who walks in integrity and lives life in accord with his [godly] beliefs—
How blessed [happy and spiritually secure] are his children after him [who have his example to follow].
AMP.8:
A [discerning] king who sits on the throne of judgment
Sifts all evil [like chaff] with his eyes [and cannot be easily fooled].
AMP.9:
Who can say, "I have cleansed my heart,
I am pure from my sin?"
AMP.10:
Differing weights [one for buying and another for selling] and differing measures,
Both of them are detestable and offensive to the LORD.
AMP.11:
Even a boy is known and distinguished by his acts,
Whether his conduct is pure and right.
AMP.12:
The hearing ear and the seeing eye,
The [omnipotent] LORD has made both of them.
AMP.13:
Do not love [excessive] sleep, or you will become poor;
Open your eyes [so that you can do your work] and you will be satisfied with bread.
AMP.14:
"It is [almost] worthless, it is [almost] worthless," says the buyer [as he negotiates the price];
But when he goes his way, then he boasts [about his bargain].
AMP.15:
There is gold, and an abundance of pearls,
But the lips of knowledge are a vessel of preciousness [the most precious of all].
AMP.16:
[The judge tells the creditor], "Take the clothes of one who is surety for a stranger;
And hold him in pledge [when he guarantees a loan] for foreigners."
AMP.17:
Food gained by deceit is sweet to a man,
But afterward his mouth will be filled with gravel [just as sin may be sweet at first, but later its consequences bring despair].
AMP.18:
Plans are established by counsel;
So make war [only] with wise guidance.
AMP.19:
He who goes about as a gossip reveals secrets;
Therefore do not associate with a gossip [who talks freely or flatters].
AMP.20:
Whoever curses his father or his mother,
His lamp [of life] will be extinguished in time of darkness.
AMP.21:
An inheritance hastily gained [by greedy, unjust means] at the beginning
Will not be blessed in the end.
AMP.22:
Do not say, "I will repay evil";
Wait [expectantly] for the LORD, and He will rescue and save you.
AMP.23:
Differing weights are detestable and offensive to the LORD,
And fraudulent scales are not good.
AMP.24:
Man's steps are ordered and ordained by the LORD.
How then can a man [fully] understand his way?
AMP.25:
It is a trap for a man to [ speak a vow of consecration and] say rashly, "It is holy!"
And [not until] afterward consider [whether he can fulfill it].
AMP.26:
A wise king sifts out the wicked [from among the good]
And drives the [threshing] wheel over them [to separate the chaff from the grain].
AMP.27:
The spirit (conscience) of man is the lamp of the LORD,
Searching and examining all the innermost parts of his being.
AMP.28:
Loyalty and mercy, truth and faithfulness, protect the king,
And he upholds his throne by lovingkindness.
AMP.29:
The glory of young men is their [physical] strength,
And the honor of aged men is their gray head [representing wisdom and experience].
AMP.30:
Blows that wound cleanse away evil,
And strokes reach to the innermost parts.

MSG.1:
Wine makes you mean, beer makes you quarrelsome—
a staggering drunk is not much fun.
MSG.2:
Quick-tempered leaders are like mad dogs—
cross them and they bite your head off.
MSG.3:
It's a mark of good character to avert quarrels,
but fools love to pick fights.
MSG.4:
A farmer too lazy to plant in the spring
has nothing to harvest in the fall.
MSG.5:
Knowing what is right is like deep water in the heart;
a wise person draws from the well within.
MSG.6:
Lots of people claim to be loyal and loving,
but where on earth can you find one?
MSG.7:
God-loyal people, living honest lives,
make it much easier for their children.
MSG.8:
Leaders who know their business and care
keep a sharp eye out for the shoddy and cheap,
MSG.9:
For who among us can be trusted
to be always diligent and honest?
MSG.10:
Switching price tags and padding the expense account
are two things God hates.
MSG.11:
Young people eventually reveal by their actions
if their motives are on the up and up.
MSG.12:
Ears that hear and eyes that see—
we get our basic equipment from God!
MSG.13:
Don't be too fond of sleep; you'll end up in the poorhouse.
Wake up and get up; then there'll be food on the table.
MSG.14:
The shopper says, "That's junk—I'll take it off your hands,"
then goes off boasting of the bargain.
MSG.15:
Drinking from the beautiful chalice of knowledge
is better than adorning oneself with gold and rare gems.
MSG.16:
Hold tight to collateral on any loan to a stranger;
beware of accepting what a transient has pawned.
MSG.17:
Stolen bread tastes sweet,
but soon your mouth is full of gravel.
MSG.18:
Form your purpose by asking for counsel,
then carry it out using all the help you can get.
MSG.19:
Gossips can't keep secrets,
so never confide in blabbermouths.
MSG.20:
Anyone who curses father and mother
extinguishes light and exists benighted.
MSG.21:
A bonanza at the beginning
is no guarantee of blessing at the end.
MSG.22:
Don't ever say, "I'll get you for that!"
Wait for God; he'll settle the score.
MSG.23:
God hates cheating in the marketplace;
rigged scales are an outrage.
MSG.24:
The very steps we take come from God;
otherwise how would we know where we're going?
MSG.25:
An impulsive vow is a trap;
later you'll wish you could get out of it.
MSG.26:
After careful scrutiny, a wise leader
makes a clean sweep of rebels and dolts.
MSG.27:
God is in charge of human life,
watching and examining us inside and out.
MSG.28:
Love and truth form a good leader;
sound leadership is founded on loving integrity.
MSG.29:
Youth may be admired for vigor,
but gray hair gives prestige to old age.
MSG.30:
A good thrashing purges evil;
punishment goes deep within us.

NIV.1:
Wine is a mocker and beer a brawler;
whoever is led astray by them is not wise.
NIV.2:
A king’s wrath is like the roar of a lion;
he who angers him forfeits his life.
NIV.3:
It is to a man’s honor to avoid strife,
but every fool is quick to quarrel.
NIV.4:
A sluggard does not plow in season;
so at harvest time he looks but finds nothing.
NIV.5:
The purposes of a man’s heart are deep waters,
but a man of understanding draws them out.
NIV.6:
Many a man claims to have unfailing love,
but a faithful man who can find?
NIV.7:
The righteous man leads a blameless life;
blessed are his children after him.
NIV.8:
When a king sits on his throne to judge,
he winnows out all evil with his eyes.
NIV.9:
Who can say, “I have kept my heart pure;
I am clean and without sin”?
NIV.10:
Differing weights and differing measures—
the Lord detests them both.
NIV.11:
Even a child is known by his actions,
by whether his conduct is pure and right.
NIV.12:
Ears that hear and eyes that see—
the Lord has made them both.
NIV.13:
Do not love sleep or you will grow poor;
stay awake and you will have food to spare.
NIV.14:
“It’s no good, it’s no good!” says the buyer;
then off he goes and boasts about his purchase.
NIV.15:
Gold there is, and rubies in abundance,
but lips that speak knowledge are a rare jewel.
NIV.16:
Take the garment of one who puts up security for a stranger;
hold it in pledge if he does it for a wayward woman.
NIV.17:
Food gained by fraud tastes sweet to a man,
but he ends up with a mouth full of gravel.
NIV.18:
Make plans by seeking advice;
if you wage war, obtain guidance.
NIV.19:
A gossip betrays a confidence;
so avoid a man who talks too much.
NIV.20:
If a man curses his father or mother,
his lamp will be snuffed out in pitch darkness.
NIV.21:
An inheritance quickly gained at the beginning
will not be blessed at the end.
NIV.22:
Do not say, “I’ll pay you back for this wrong!”
Wait for the Lord, and he will deliver you.
NIV.23:
The Lord detests differing weights,
and dishonest scales do not please him.
NIV.24:
A man’s steps are directed by the Lord.
How then can anyone understand his own way?
NIV.25:
It is a trap for a man to dedicate something rashly
and only later to consider his vows.
NIV.26:
A wise king winnows out the wicked;
he drives the threshing wheel over them.
NIV.27:
The lamp of the Lord searches the spirit of a man;
it searches out his inmost being.
NIV.28:
Love and faithfulness keep a king safe;
through love his throne is made secure.
NIV.29:
The glory of young men is their strength,
gray hair the splendor of the old.
NIV.30:
Blows and wounds cleanse away evil,
and beatings purge the inmost being.

NRSVCE.1:
Wine is a mocker, strong drink a brawler,
and whoever is led astray by it is not wise.
NRSVCE.2:
The dread anger of a king is like the growling of a lion;
anyone who provokes him to anger forfeits life itself.
NRSVCE.3:
It is honorable to refrain from strife,
but every fool is quick to quarrel.
NRSVCE.4:
The lazy person does not plow in season;
harvest comes, and there is nothing to be found.
NRSVCE.5:
The purposes in the human mind are like deep water,
but the intelligent will draw them out.
NRSVCE.6:
Many proclaim themselves loyal,
but who can find one worthy of trust?
NRSVCE.7:
The righteous walk in integrity —
happy are the children who follow them!
NRSVCE.8:
A king who sits on the throne of judgment
winnows all evil with his eyes.
NRSVCE.9:
Who can say, “I have made my heart clean;
I am pure from my sin”?
NRSVCE.10:
Diverse weights and diverse measures
are both alike an abomination to the LORD.
NRSVCE.11:
Even children make themselves known by their acts,
by whether what they do is pure and right.
NRSVCE.12:
The hearing ear and the seeing eye —
the LORD has made them both.
NRSVCE.13:
Do not love sleep, or else you will come to poverty;
open your eyes, and you will have plenty of bread.
NRSVCE.14:
“Bad, bad,” says the buyer,
then goes away and boasts.
NRSVCE.15:
There is gold, and abundance of costly stones;
but the lips informed by knowledge are a precious jewel.
NRSVCE.16:
Take the garment of one who has given surety for a stranger;
seize the pledge given as surety for foreigners.
NRSVCE.17:
Bread gained by deceit is sweet,
but afterward the mouth will be full of gravel.
NRSVCE.18:
Plans are established by taking advice;
wage war by following wise guidance.
NRSVCE.19:
A gossip reveals secrets;
therefore do not associate with a babbler.
NRSVCE.20:
If you curse father or mother,
your lamp will go out in utter darkness.
NRSVCE.21:
An estate quickly acquired in the beginning
will not be blessed in the end.
NRSVCE.22:
Do not say, “I will repay evil”;
wait for the LORD, and he will help you.
NRSVCE.23:
Differing weights are an abomination to the LORD,
and false scales are not good.
NRSVCE.24:
All our steps are ordered by the LORD;
how then can we understand our own ways?
NRSVCE.25:
It is a snare for one to say rashly, “It is holy,”
and begin to reflect only after making a vow.
NRSVCE.26:
A wise king winnows the wicked,
and drives the wheel over them.
NRSVCE.27:
The human spirit is the lamp of the LORD,
searching every inmost part.
NRSVCE.28:
Loyalty and faithfulness preserve the king,
and his throne is upheld by righteousness.
NRSVCE.29:
The glory of youths is their strength,
but the beauty of the aged is their gray hair.
NRSVCE.30:
Blows that wound cleanse away evil;
beatings make clean the innermost parts.

NET.1: Wine is a mocker and strong drink is a brawler; whoever goes astray by them is not wise.
NET.2: The king’s terrifying anger is like the roar of a lion; whoever provokes him sins against himself.
NET.3: It is an honor for a person to cease from strife, but every fool quarrels.
NET.4: The sluggard will not plow during the planting season, so at harvest time he looks for the crop but has nothing.
NET.5: Counsel in a person’s heart is like deep water, but an understanding person draws it out.
NET.6: Many people profess their loyalty, but a faithful person — who can find?
NET.7: The righteous person behaves in integrity; blessed are his children after him.
NET.8: A king sitting on the throne to judge separates out all evil with his eyes.
NET.9: Who can say, “I have kept my heart clean; I am pure from my sin”?
NET.10: Diverse weights and diverse measures — the LORD abhors both of them.
NET.11: Even a young man is known by his actions, whether his activity is pure and whether it is right.
NET.12: The ear that hears and the eye that sees — the LORD has made them both.
NET.13: Do not love sleep, lest you become impoverished; open your eyes so that you might be satisfied with food.
NET.14: “It’s worthless! It’s worthless!” says the buyer, but when he goes on his way, he boasts.
NET.15: There is gold, and an abundance of rubies, but words of knowledge are like a precious jewel.
NET.16: Take a man’s garment when he has given security for a stranger, and when he gives surety for strangers, hold him in pledge.
NET.17: Bread gained by deceit tastes sweet to a person, but afterward his mouth will be filled with gravel.
NET.18: Plans are established by counsel, so make war with guidance.
NET.19: The one who goes about gossiping reveals secrets; therefore do not associate with someone who is always opening his mouth.
NET.20: The one who curses his father and his mother, his lamp will be extinguished in the blackest darkness.
NET.21: An inheritance gained easily in the beginning will not be blessed in the end.
NET.22: Do not say, “I will pay back evil!” Wait for the LORD, so that he may vindicate you.
NET.23: The LORD abhors differing weights, and dishonest scales are wicked.
NET.24: The steps of a person are ordained by the LORD — so how can anyone understand his own way?
NET.25: It is a snare for a person to rashly cry, “Holy!” and only afterward to consider what he has vowed.
NET.26: A wise king separates out the wicked; he turns the threshing wheel over them.
NET.27: The human spirit is like the lamp of the LORD, searching all his innermost parts.
NET.28: Loyal love and truth preserve a king, and his throne is upheld by loyal love.
NET.29: The glory of young men is their strength, and the splendor of old men is gray hair.
NET.30: Beatings and wounds cleanse away evil, and floggings cleanse the innermost being.
